title: Finance Analyst - Regional Science & Healthcare
description:
• Join a fast-growing regional organization in science & medical solutions
  • Work closely with cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders

About Our Client

Our client is a leading regional solutions provider in the science and medical sector, delivering end-to-end services from research and quality control to pharmaceutical production and clinical diagnostics. With a nearly five-decade track record, the company operates across Southeast Asia, combining science, medical, and project expertise to support clients and partners wherever needed. Their Singapore office is expanding and looking for a talented Finance Analyst to join the team.

Job Description

  • Prepare, consolidate, and analyse financial reports, including monthly, quarterly, and annual management reports
  • Collaborate with country and cross-functional teams on budgeting, forecasting, and business planning processes
  • Monitor key financial performance indicators, including working capital, cash flow, and treasury metrics, focusing on cash conversion cycle, DSO, DPO, and DIO
  • Develop actual and forecast cash flow reporting and analysis, ensuring alignment with project timelines and business needs
  • Support treasury operations, including cash flow projections, liquidity management, and cash optimisation strategies
  • Develop business cases, financial models, and presentations for senior management and board reviews
  • Provide insights and recommendations to support strategic decision-making, cost efficiencies, and process improvements
  • Ensure compliance with accounting standards, internal controls, and company policies, and assist in internal and external audits
  • Participate in ad hoc projects, system implementations, and continuous improvement initiatives

The Successful Applicant

  • Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, Business, or related field (local universities preferred: SMU, NTU, NUS)
  • 2-5 years of relevant experience in finance, FP&A, or treasury
  • Proficient in financial modelling, budgeting, forecasting, and MS Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
  • Strong analytical, problem-solving, and presentation skills
  • Ability to work collaboratively across teams and provide actionable insights
  • Detail-oriented with a proactive approach to process improvements

What's on Offer

  • Exposure to a fast-growing organization with regional operations
  • Frequent stakeholder interaction and senior management facetime, ability to influence commercial strategies and work with an established and transparent leadership team
  • Long-term career growth opportunities within finance and commercial business partnering
